title: [InstCombine] fold icmp ne (and X, 1), 0 --> trunc X to i1
summary:
The generalization extends a vector-specific optimization to also handle scalar cases. Originally, the pattern `icmp ne (and X, 1), 0` → `trunc X to i1` was only applied to vector types, under the assumption that scalar icmp might have better downstream analysis. The patch removes the vector-only restriction (`Cmp.getType()->isVectorTy()`) and the TODO comment, enabling the transformation for both scalars and vectors.

This demonstrates a key principle: when an optimization is logically sound for both scalar and vector types, generalize it to cover all cases unless there's a proven downside. The change simplifies the code, reduces special-case logic, and leverages existing trunc handling uniformly. Developers should look for similar artificial restrictions—especially type-based ones—and evaluate whether they can be lifted to broaden optimization impact without regressing performance.
